factors.name: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Optimism
factors.summary: Trump claims negotiations in 'final stages' and reports indicate reluctance to resume conflict unless US troops are killed (WSJ). Reduction in geopolitical risk premium should weigh on safe-haven gold demand.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: No major FOMC decision or rate guidance in this news window. Fed's Schmid cited inflation as 'biggest risk' but no dovish pivot signals. Lack of fresh real yield catalyst limits directional conviction.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) Strength
factors.summary: USD reversed earlier declines and climbed toward 99.45 in NA session; risk-on sentiment post-equity rally (Dow +1.73%, record high) supports dollar. Stronger DXY is typically headwind for gold.

factors.name: Equity Risk-On / Sentiment Rotation
factors.summary: Dow closes at record high (+1.73%); financials and mega-caps led; equity ETF inflows suggest risk appetite returning. Gold often underperforms in risk-on regimes as capital rotates into equities.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: While Iran threats persist and Hezbollah rejects ceasefire, Trump's optimism and reported de-escalation stance are reducing marginal safe-haven bids. Mixed signals from Lebanon/Israel/Ukraine but no acute crisis escalation.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ, ECB, BoE)
factors.summary: BoJ rate hike case strengthens (Japan real wages +1.9% YoY, fourth consecutive gain; BOJ meeting June 15-16). Japan Finance Minister Katayama ready to respond on FX. BoJ tightening could support yen carry unwind, but no imminent action confirmed.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP Release (Friday June 5)
factors.summary: Major NFP print due hours away; highly anticipated after soft prior prints. Depending on beat/miss, could shift Fed rate-cut odds and real yields; this is a key near-term catalyst that limits conviction until data landed.

factors.name: Bitcoin Weakness / Crypto Liquidation
factors.summary: Bitcoin down 13% on the week (worst since Feb), with 13-day record ETF outflow streak. Liquidity rotation out of 'risk-on' alternatives (BTC, leveraged positions) into safe assets could compete with gold for safe-haven flows.

factors.name: Oil Price Stability (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil fell 3% on Trump reluctance to restart Iran conflict; now stabilizing. Lower oil removes inflation concerns that would otherwise support gold via real-yield compression.

factors.name: Gold Price Action: 4h / 24h Change
factors.summary: Gold down only -0.40% in 4h and -0.38% in 24h — extremely tight consolidation. No clear momentum in either direction; move has NOT extended. Suggests market indecision ahead of NFP.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating tightly (+/-0.40% in 4h) amid competing narratives: geopolitical risk premium is eroding due to Trump's ceasefire optimism and reduced Iran conflict fears (risk-on equity rally, DXY strength), but real yields remain range-bound and NFP data is imminent. The market is in a 'wait and see' mode ahead of Friday's employment report, which will likely reset Fed rate-cut expectations and real yield expectations. Safe-haven demand is soft while equity risk appetite is strong, but gold is not yet sold off sharply — suggesting institutional positioning remains mixed.
catalysts_ahead: US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) – Friday June 5, 2026 (IMMINENT, ~1-24h away)
BoJ Policy Meeting – June 15–16, 2026 (potential rate hike signal)
US-Iran ceasefire deal outcome (could resolve in 24–72h per Trump comments)
Potential Hezbollah/Israel escalation if ceasefire collapses
screening.risk_warning: If NFP beats expectations and real yields spike sharply higher (via Fed rate-cut probability collapsing), or if DXY breaks above 99.50 on sustained risk-on, gold could test $4,450–$4,400 support. Conversely, if geopolitical tensions re-escalate (Iran military response, Hezbollah attack) or NFP disappoints (dovish for Fed), gold could reclaim $4,520+ quickly. The binary outcome of NFP in <24h makes this inherently choppy — avoid overcommitting until data lands.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extremely tight with no directional bias yet (PHASE 2 chop). Wait for NFP release and resulting DXY/real-yield reaction before committing. If gold breaks above $4,510 on dovish NFP, consider small longs toward $4,530; if breaks below $4,470 on hot NFP, watch $4,450 support.
